sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: McDonald’s Corporation (MCD): Our Calculation of Intrinsic Value
feed.summary: This article presents a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis for McDonald's Corporation (MCD), calculating an intrinsic value of $95 per share. Despite the company's strong global brand, robust cash flows, and excellent operational durability, the current market price of around $319 suggests it is significantly overvalued by approximately 70% based on conservative assumptions. The analysis highlights McDonald's status as a high-quality consumer business but cautions against the limited margin of safety at its present valuation.

feed.title: Does FDA’s UPLIZNA gMG Expansion Reshape the Bull Case for Amgen’s Diversification Story (AMGN)?
feed.summary: The FDA's approval of Amgen's UPLIZNA for generalized myasthenia gravis, targeting AChR- and MuSK-antibody positive adults, significantly boosts the company's autoimmune portfolio. This expansion supports Amgen's strategy to diversify revenue beyond its established bone-health and oncology franchises, reinforcing its investment narrative amidst ongoing biosimilar and pricing pressures. While the approval strengthens Amgen's pipeline breadth, its long-term impact will depend on how effectively it offsets challenges from maturing legacy products and regulatory scrutiny on drug prices.

feed.title: Sands China units renew to end of 2028 trademark and marketing services deals with Las Vegas Sands parent
feed.summary: Sands China Ltd. has renewed trademark and marketing services agreements with its U.S. parent, Las Vegas Sands Corp., until December 31, 2028. Four Macau-registered Sands China subsidiaries will pay an annual royalty of 1.5 percent of gross non-gaming and gaming revenue for trademark use, with annual caps set for 2026, 2027, and 2028. The renewals are crucial for the marketing and corporate identity of Sands China's businesses in Macau.

feed.title: Branding deal for MGM China to double percentage of adjusted consolidated net monthly revenues payable for MGM brand use
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International, the majority owner of MGM China Holdings Ltd, has announced a new "long-term branding agreement" which will double the licensing fee MGM China pays for using the 'MGM' brand, from 1.75 percent to 3.5 percent of adjusted consolidated net monthly revenues. This new agreement, effective January 1, 2026, will run until 2032 and is subject to an annual cap, estimated at US$188 million for full-year 2026, with MGM Resorts retaining 67% and Pansy Ho Chiu King retaining the balance. The deal is meant to secure MGM China's "most important intangible asset" and provides MGM Resorts with "fair compensation" for brand use.
